title: pellaea paupercula
intro: Pellaea paupercula, also known as the poor man's fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pellaea paupercula include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Pellaea paupercula can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
